name: Google Jules

form_factor.comment: Hosted async coding agent connected to GitHub repositories. Per-form-factor links: Web: https://jules.google/.

latest_major_update.comment: Current site advertises Gemini 3 Pro access and tiered concurrent task limits.

rules.comment: No public docs found for persistent repo instruction files. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

skills.comment: No reusable skill/playbook system found. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

hooks.comment: GitHub issue label assignment acts as a lightweight trigger.

mcp_servers.comment: No public MCP server configuration found. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

custom_commands.comment: Prompt and issue assignment workflow, not user-defined slash commands. Location: hosted/web/API-managed; no local disk folder documented.

goal_command.comment: Jules is a hosted async agent for GitHub issues/PRs; no interactive `/goal` slash command in public docs.

subagents.comment: Plan tiers advertise multi-agent development and high concurrent task counts.

model_selection.comment: Tier-dependent model access; not arbitrary model selection.

approval_mode.comment: User reviews plan/diff and approves changes/PR.

sandbox_mode.comment: Work happens in a Cloud VM clone.

resume.comment: Async task history exists in the hosted product, but no explicit session resume docs found.

continue.comment: Designed for async continuation in hosted tasks; no CLI continue command.

non_interactive.comment: Can be triggered from GitHub issues, but not a general local headless CLI.

output_format.comment: Outputs plans, diffs, and PRs; no general machine-readable format found.

hosted_agent.comment: Hosted async agent running in Google-managed Cloud VMs.

custom_model_provider.comment: Uses Gemini models; BYO provider not documented.

pricing.value: Free 15 tasks/day; Pro 100 tasks/day; Ultra 300 tasks/day
pricing.comment: Site lists daily task and concurrency limits by tier.

notes: Best categorized as an async hosted coding worker rather than a terminal pair-programmer. Strong on GitHub PR flow, approvals, Cloud VM isolation, and concurrency.

transcripts.comment: Location: hosted/web/API-managed session history; no local transcript directory documented.
